page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-4 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server features 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is configured with 16 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor includes 32 KB of L1 instruction cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, including VNNI and BF16, and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile characterized by strong single-core capabilities but limited multi-core scaling and memory throughput. The instance achieves a strong rating in stress-ng single-core CPU performance and geekbench text processing. However, multi-core benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, fall into the lower performance tier. Memory bandwidth is also a notable constraint, with both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached reads and writes scoring in the bottom 25%. Similarly, database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput are relatively weak.

Given its resource density and performance characteristics, the c3d-standard-4 is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that rely primarily on single-threaded execution or text processing rather than memory-intensive or highly parallelized tasks. It is suitable for lightweight general-purpose applications, basic text processing, single-threaded scripting, and small-scale LLM inference using models under 135M parameters. It is less suitable for large-scale database operations, high-throughput web serving, or medium-to-large language model inference due to significant performance degradation on larger models.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor on the x86_64 architecture. It utilizes 2 physical cores with hyper-threading and provides 4.0 GB of memory per core, with no local storage or GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU execution and text processing capabilities, contrasted by weaker multi-core scaling and low memory bandwidth. The instance supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for single-threaded tasks and lightweight applications. It is best suited for basic text processing, single-threaded scripting, and small-scale language model inference, while showing performance limitations in database operations and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-4 is a general-purpose instance with 4 vCPUs and 16 GB RAM. It features strong single-core CPU performance but exhibits limited multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-4 is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor.
tagline: A general-purpose virtual machine featuring strong single-core processing and balanced memory allocation for lightweight, single-threaded workloads.

Strong single-core CPU and text processing performance
Supports AVX-512, VNNI, BF16, and AES instructions
Limited multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth
No local storage or bundled GPU accelerators
